name: xlns

metadata.name: xlns
metadata.summary: A configurable python package for Logarithmic Number System eXperimentation

documentation:
# xlns
XLNS: a configurable python package for Logarithmic Number System eXperimentation


# Getting Started

It is recommended to create a python virtual environment for experimentation with the ``xlns`` package. Within that environment, ensure you have the dependencies listed below installed.

Explanatory information about LNS is in the ``LNS_Intro.md`` file.  By default,  ``xlns`` uses 64-bit built-in floating point (FP) to calculate as accurately as is possible.  We call this approach _ideal_. The recent interest in LNS is mostly because there are many _non-ideal_ approximations which greatly reduce the cost of the hardware, and some of these methods are available here as _user configurations_.  Information on available user configurations can be found in the ``README.md`` file in ``src/xlnsconf``.  It also explains how you can contribute your own configurations to this open-source project.

# Dependencies
numpy

````
pip3 install numpy
````

For some examples, matplotlib is also needed:
````
pip3 install matplotlib
````

You can grab all the dependencies in one go using the requirements file thus:
````
pip install -r requirements.txt
````

modules.name: xlnsconf.xlnsudFracnorm
documentation:
user-defined LNS addition via Fractional Normalization

importing this changes xlnsud ("user defined") class to implement 
one of two versions of LNS Add by Fractional Normalization:
   Original ("pal") by Tsiaras and Paliouras in ISCAS 2017 
   Improved ("han") by Zhang, Han, et al. in GLSVLSI 2024
Needed because above don't use Gaussian Logs (sb/db)
other variations in Han (guards, approx fxpt +) not impl

modules.name: xlnsconf.lpvip_ufunc
documentation: several versions LPVIP (Arnold, PATMOS 2004) using Mitchell's method as Gaussian Log

modules.name: xlnsconf.dally
documentation:
implements Dally et al. bin-based LNS summation

this code for academic non-commercial use only
may be covered by U.S. Patent Application Publication US20210056446A1
to use:

> import xlns as xl
> import xlnsconf.dally
> xl.xlnsnp.sum = xlnsconf.dally.sum_xlnsnp
> xl.xlnsnpv.sum = xlnsconf.dally.sum_xlnsnpv

to restore default:

: xl.xlnsnp.sum = xl.xlnsnp.sum_default
  xl.xlnsnpv.sum = xl.xlnsnpv.sum_default

an example:
\>>> import xlns as xl
\>>> import xlnsconf.dally
\>>> x=xl.xlnsnpv([1,2,3,4.],8)
\>>> x.sum()
xlnsnpv(10.015890222930839 f=8)
\>>> xl.xlnsnpv.sum = xlnscoonf.dally.sum_xlnsnpv
\>>> x.sum()
xlnsnpv(9.988807817513946 f=8)
\>>> x=xl.xlnsnpv([1,2,3,4.,5,6,7,11],8)
\>>> x.sum()
xlnsnpv(38.99335135447898 f=8)
\>>> xl.xlnsnpv.sum = xl.xlnsnpv.sum_default
\>>> x.sum()
xlnsnpv(38.99335135447898 f=8)

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb
documentation: interpolate sb with no guards, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_cotrdb
documentation: ideal sb, flawed implementation of cotran db (doesn't handle special cases)

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsbcotrdb
documentation: interpolate sb with no guards, correct cotran db using same interpolate
actually do need z1==0 case because otherwise min cotr error 10x too big

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g2extra
documentation: interpolate sb with 2 guards and extra table, ideal db
with both special cases (z1==0 and z2==0), errors of interp sb+cotran db are reasonable

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g4extra
documentation: interpolate sb with 4 guards and extra table, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g6extra
documentation: interpolate sb with 6 guards and extra table, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g4
documentation: interpolate sb with 4 guards, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g6
documentation: interpolate sb with 4 guards, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsb_g2
documentation: interpolate sb with 2 guards, ideal db

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsbcotrdb_g2
documentation: interpolate sb with 2 guards, correct cotran db with same interpolate

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsbcotrdb_g4
documentation: interpolate sb with 4 guards, correct cotran db with same interpolate

functions.name: xlnsconf.interp_cotran_ufunc.sbdb_ufunc_interpsbcotrdb_g6
documentation: interpolate sb with 6 guards, correct cotran db with same interpolate

modules.name: xlns
documentation:
for eXperimentation with Logarithmic Number System  

classes offer:
 scalar (xlns,xlnsr,xlnsv,xlnsb and xlnsud) 
 array (xlnsnp, xlnsnpr, xlnsnpv and xlnsnpb)
 non-redundant (xlns,xlnsv,xlnsb,xlnsud,xlnsnp,xlnsnpv and xlnsnpb)
 redundant (xlnsr, xlnsnpr)
 global precision with xlnssetF (xlns, xlnsr, xlnsud, xlnsnp, xlnsnpr)
 per instance precision (xlnsv, xlnsb, xlnsnpv, xlnsnpb)
 non-integer precision ignore xlnsF (xlnsb, xlnsnpb)

functions.name: xlns.xlnssetovf
documentation: set min and max overflow values

functions.name: xlns.xlnssetF
documentation:
set global xlnsF and corresponding xlnsB = 2**2**(-xlnsF)

all logarithms internally use the global base, xlnsB, or its local equivalent, B
this function calculates this given an integer F so that using the internal base
is isomorphic to a base-two logarithm with F bits of fractional precision
the default is F=23

functions.name: xlns.sbdb_ufunc_ideal
documentation: default ufunc for ideal Gaussian log for all add,sub; may substitute user supplied sbdb_ufunc

functions.name: xlns.float64
documentation: convert to floating point; like np.float64, but works with all classes

functions.name: xlns.xlnscopy
documentation: deep copy of list converting to xlns (or type and precision specified by 2nd and 3rd args)

functions.name: xlns.XXXxlnsrcopy
documentation: elif isinstance(y,int) or isinstance(y,float) or isinstance(y,np.float64) or isinstance(y,np.float32) or isinstance(y,xlns) or isinstance(y,xlnsr) or isinstance(y,xlnsnp) or isinstance(y,xlnsnpr) or isinstance(y,xlnsnpv) or isinstance(y,xlnsv) or isinstance(y,xlnsb):
all the rest of xlns*copy are deprecated, and will be removed

classes.name: xlns.xlns
documentation: simple scalar LNS using global base xlnsB

classes.name: xlns.xlnsnp

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnp.xlns
documentation: convert xlnsnp to similar-shaped list of xlns

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnp.ovf
documentation: return copy except for values that underflow/overflow, which are clipped according to xlnssetovf

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnp.conjugate
documentation: end of those not found in numpy

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnp.sum
documentation: divide and conquer summation that reorders operands to minimize __add__ calls
numpy-like array of non-redundant LNS using global base xlnsB

classes.name: xlns.xlnsr
documentation: scalar redundant LNS using global base xlnsB

classes.name: xlns.xlnsnpr

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpr.xlns
documentation: convert xlnsnpr to similar-shaped list of xlns
ovf does not make sense for xlnsnpr

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpr.__matmul__
documentation: v must be not be xlnsnpr

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpr.__rmatmul__
documentation: v must be not be xlnsnpr
numpy-like array of redundant LNS using global base xlnsB

classes.name: xlns.xlnsud
documentation: user-defined subclass of xlns; allows xlnsud__add__ to be changed from Gaussian log to frac norm

classes.name: xlns.xlnsv
documentation: scalar non-redundant LNS using distinct int precision F (unrelated to xlnsF) on each instance

classes.name: xlns.xlnsnpv

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpv.xlns
documentation: convert xlnsnpv to similar-shaped list of xlnsv

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpv.ovf
documentation: return copy except for values that underflow/overflow, which are clipped according to xlnssetovf

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpv.__repr__
documentation: end of those not found in numpy
numpy-like array of non-redundant LNS using int precision f <= xlnsF on each instance

classes.name: xlns.xlnsb
documentation: scalar non-redundant LNS whose precision on each instance defined by arbitrary real B>1

classes.name: xlns.xlnsnpb

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpb.xlns
documentation: convert xlnsnpb to similar-shaped list of xlnsb

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpb.ovf
documentation: return copy except for values that underflow/overflow, which are clipped according to xlnssetovf

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpb.conjugate
documentation: end of those not found in numpy

methods.name: xlns.xlnsnpb.__mul__
documentation: if isinstance(v,xlnsv) or isinstance(v,xlnsb) or isinstance(v,xlnsnpb):
numpy-like array of non-redundant LNS whose precision on each instance defined by real B>1
